text: MarketReel — Global Film Distribution Intelligence JapanFilm Censorship & Certification GuidelinesEirin (映倫) — Motion Picture Code of Ethics Committee — Regulatory Intelligence1. Regulatory AuthorityFilm classification in Japan is administered by Eirin (映倫管理委員会), an independent self-regulatory body established in 1949. Japan's constitution provides strong free expression protections — there is no government censorship of film content in the Western sense. However, Eirin classifications carry significant commercial weight and social authority. Cinema chains universally enforce Eirin ratings. The primary legal constraint is obscenity law (Penal Code §175), which prohibits display of genitalia without pixelation in any commercially distributed content.2. Eirin Rating SystemEirin RatingAudienceDescriptionAgent Decision RuleGAll agesSuitable for general audiences including young childrenFull release, unrestricted. Maximum marketing reach.PG12Under 12 with parental guidanceParental guidance recommended for under-12Family release with advisory. Mild content.R1515 and aboveRestricted to age 15 and above; ID check requiredAge-restricted marketing. Under-15s cannot attend.R1818 and aboveStrictly restricted to adults 18; strict ID verificationAdult content. Restricted venues. No under-18 marketing.3. Legal ProhibitionsGenitalia shown without mosaic or pixelation — Penal Code §175 (obscenity law) prohibits this in ANY commercially distributed content in Japan, regardless of artistic intent or context. Mosaic or blur is a mandatory legal requirement.Child Sexual Abuse Material — criminal prohibition under the Act on Punishment of Activities Relating to Child Prostitution and Child Pornography (1999, amended 2014).Content constituting criminal defamation of named real individuals under Japanese civil and criminal law.AGENT LEGAL RULEGenitalia pixelation is not optional in Japan — it is a legal requirement under Penal Code §175. Any film containing non-pixelated genitalia MUST be modified before Japan release. This applies to all content regardless of artistic merit or international critical reception.4. Conditional ContentContent CategoryEirin Rating ImpactAgent RuleViolence — action or fantasyPG12–R15Standard action: PG12. Realistic graphic violence with blood: R15. Extreme torture or gore: R18.Sexual content — non-explicitR15–R18Implied sex or non-nude scenes: PG12–R15. Partial nudity: R15. Explicit sex: R18.Nudity — non-sexual artisticR15Nudity without sexual framing: R15 typically. Genitalia: legally requires mosaic regardless of context.Drug useR15–R18Drug use depicted: R15. Instruction or glamourisation: R18. Japan's drug laws are extremely strict — cannabis is treated equivalently to hard narcotics.LGBTQ contentNo special restrictionJapan has no legal restriction on LGBTQ content. Rating is based on sexual content level only, not orientation.Suicide or self-harmR15–R18Japan has specific media guidelines on suicide depiction (Suicide Countermeasures Promotion Act context). Detailed method depiction: R18. Consult Eirin for films with prominent suicide themes.Horror — psychologicalR15Psychological horror: R15 standard. J-horror genre precedent well established.WWII Pacific War — US triumphalismHIGH cultural sensitivityNot legally restricted but commercial backlash risk is significant. See Cultural Notes section.Japanese Emperor or Imperial Family depicted fictionallyHIGH cultural sensitivityCommercially non-viable due to extreme cultural sensitivity. Not legally prohibited per se.5. Cultural Distribution NotesWWII and Hiroshima or Nagasaki content: Films depicting the atomic bombings require careful handling. Positive or triumphalist American framing carries significant commercial backlash risk in Japan. The Oppenheimer Japan release (2023) faced significant local controversy. Flag any Pacific War content for Japan-specific PR strategy.Imperial Army war crimes (Nanjing Massacre, Unit 731): Highly polarising in Japan. Nationalist domestic press will respond strongly to detailed depictions. Not illegal but commercially disruptive.Japanese cultural caricature or yellowface: Growing commercial sensitivity. Flag for marketing review.Earthquake or tsunami disaster content: Japan experiences frequent seismic events. Disaster content featuring Japan can be either deeply resonant or inappropriately timed — assess release window against recent events.6. Localisation RequirementsJapanese subtitle quality is critical — audiences have high expectations. Poor or culturally tone-deaf subtitles generate mainstream press criticism.Honorific and social register levels in Japanese dialogue dubs must accurately reflect character social status relationships.Comedy and irony translation: Physical and slapstick comedy translates well; verbal irony and cultural in-jokes require skilled localisation.7. Agent Decision Framework — JapanTrigger Detected in FilmRisk LevelRecommended ActionGenitalia shown (any context)HIGH — LEGAL REQUIREMENTMandatory mosaic or pixelation for Japan release. No exceptions. Legal requirement under Penal Code §175.WWII US triumphalism related to Pacific WarMEDIUM–HIGH culturalFlag for Japan-specific marketing review. Consider tonal adjustment in Japan PR materials.LGBTQ romantic contentLOWNo legal restriction. Rate based on sexual content level only.Drug use (especially cannabis)MEDIUM–HIGHJapan's strict drug laws make even cannabis depiction sensitive. Add negative consequence framing.Suicide method depicted in detailHIGHConsult Eirin directly. R18 likely. Japan's suicide awareness context makes this highly sensitive.Japanese Emperor or Imperial Family fictionalisedHIGH culturalCommercially non-viable. Flag for complete removal or alternative framing.Graphic violenceMEDIUMR15 standard. Extreme gore: R18.Explicit sexual contentMEDIUMR18. Genitalia: mosaic legally required.Japanese cultural caricature or mockeryMEDIUM culturalCommercial backlash risk. Flag for marketing review.MarketReel Confidential Japan Censorship Guidelines For Agent Reasoning & Internal Distribution Use Only
